title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for suicide prevention after the loss of 2 friends
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kerrie Jacoby is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor two friends lost to suicide.
Jacoby aims to raise awareness for mental health and support Samaritans' suicide prevention efforts.
The marathon takes place in April, the same month she lost her friend Tyler to suicide.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A Southbridge woman in her 60s died after being rescued from a house fire on South St. Monday afternoon.
The victim was moved from Southbridge to UMass Memorial-Harrington Hospital and later died at Mass General.
Officials reported no working fire alarms in the home; multiple accidental fire causes were found in the kitchen.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launches a 'call a boomer' phone line in Brookline to connect youth with seniors in Reno.
The project aims to bridge generational gaps through conversations between Boston University students and Nevada seniors.
Recorded conversations may be shared on social media; initial run scheduled for one month outside Pavement Coffeehouse.

title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Gas prices in Massachusetts have risen sharply due to disruption in the global oil market caused by the conflict with Iran.
Regular gas in Massachusetts jumped 50 cents to $3.40 per gallon, with diesel prices climbing to $4.83 per gallon.
Massachusetts' reliance on imported fuel and lack of local refineries leaves the region vulnerable to global oil price fluctuations.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Patriots agree to sign WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns with the Packers during the 2025 season.
The signing follows the Patriots releasing Stefon Diggs, aiming to strengthen their passing game for QB Drake Maye.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers $750 bonus to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston, for entire home rentals.
Boston hosts expected to earn $5,200 on average, with World Cup Airbnb demand up 80% this year.
World Cup matches will occur at rebranded Boston Stadium in Foxborough from June 11 to July 19.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, 53, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Rowan, a Massachusetts native, has familial ties to Wellesley where he watched the marathon as a child.
Running honors his grandmother and mother, both affected by dementia, and raises awareness for Alzheimer's research.

title: Fundraiser launched for Reddit tipster who helped identify suspect in Brown shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fundraiser has been launched for 'John,' a Reddit user who helped identify the Brown shooting suspect.
John, who is currently homeless, tipped investigators about a Nissan sedan linked to Claudio Neves Valente.
The fundraiser, organized by Ceremony café's Chris Hoffman and Michelle Cheng, aims to raise $50,000 for John's stability.

title: Joe Mazzulla praises Baylor Scheierman’s work ethic
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Joe Mazzulla praised Baylor Scheierman's competitiveness and work ethic following the Celtics' 109-98 win over the Cavaliers.
Scheierman achieved a double-double against the Cavaliers, scoring 16 points and grabbing 10 rebounds in 26 minutes.
Despite a recent thumb fracture, Scheierman has appeared in 59 games this season, averaging 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds per game.

title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere Police are searching for a suspect in three indecent assaults near Beachmont MBTA Station from January to March.
The suspect is a Hispanic male, 5-foot-4 to 5-foot-7, with a thin build and short dark hair.
Residents are urged to check surveillance footage and stay alert; contact Revere Police at 781-286-1212 with information.
